title: Cayratia papillata
intro: Cayratia papillata, also known as the rough creeper vine, is a perennial climber native to tropical regions of Asia and Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its health benefits such as antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cayratia papillata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cayratia papillata is commonly prepared as a decoction or infused as a tea, and it is also used in the form of a syrup or a tincture for treating various health conditions.
taxonomy: Cayratia papillata is classified within the family Vitaceae and is a member of the genus Cayratia. This genus is a part of the tribe Viteae. The family Vitaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as grape or wine family. The plant is native to tropical Africa and is also found in other parts of the continent. Cayratia papillata is an evergreen climber that uses its tendrils to support itself in its environment.
